dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, my loved one left weeks ago and never arrived at their destination. Nobody's seen them since.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The last anyone heard, they were camped {location_hint} when trouble hit. I found the campsite a week later: torn apart, supplies scattered, but no body. {target_npc} thinks the {enemy_type_plural} might have taken them. Before anything else, I need you to retrieve {quest_item} from the campsite. It is the only thing that can tell us which way they went.
dialogueChunks.outro: They're out there somewhere. I can feel it. I just need someone brave enough to {quest_action}. {quest_origin} started all of this. {quest_reward_hint} would be my undying gratitude.

dialogueChunks.intro: Please, {player_name}, you have to help me. My child wandered off during the {enemy_type} attack and nobody's seen them since.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: When the {enemy_type_plural} hit, everyone scattered. In the chaos, my child was separated from the group. {target_npc} saw them running toward {location_hint}, but nobody could follow because the {enemy_type_plural} were between us. {target_npc} is the last person who saw my child alive. Please, go speak with them and find out exactly where they went.
dialogueChunks.outro: They're young and alone in the wild. Every hour counts. {quest_stakes_The} {quest_stakes_is} all I can think about. Before {quest_time_pressure}.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I've been looking for someone for a very long time. And I think I've finally found a lead.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} and I built this place together. Years ago, they left to establish a route through {location_hint} and promised to return. That promise turned into years of silence. Last week, a trader recognized {quest_item} I described: said they saw it in a camp near the edge of {enemy_type} territory. I need you to go there and bring {quest_item} back to me.
dialogueChunks.outro: {target_npc} might be alive, might be captive, might be something I haven't considered. Either way, I owe it to them to {quest_action}. For the sake of {quest_stakes_the}, I have to try. {quest_origin} bound us together.
